summary: Binomial coefficient C(n,k) on u64 via a tail-recursive product accumulator `binomial_loop` + a single-step self-recursion at the `k > n - k` symmetry redirect, composing five helpers (trailing_zeros → Stein gcd_stein_loop → gcd_u64 outer wrapper → multiply_and_divide → binomial_loop); the loop invariant `r_d = C(n_init, d - 1)` is carried through `multiply_and_divide` per iteration via the multiplicative step identity `(j+1)·C(n,j+1) = C(n,j)·(n-j)` (proved by structural induction on n,j), which simultaneously discharges multiply_and_divide's `b ∣ r * a` precondition and its `r*a/b < 2^64` no-overflow precondition; multiply_and_divide itself proved by a coprime-gcd cancellation identity (`mad_value_param` via `Nat.coprime_div_gcd_div_gcd`) — the natural-but-exponential recursive Pascal-triangle oracle `nchoose` is shadowed by an iteratively-computable list-based surrogate `nchooseFast` (via `pascalRow`/`pascalNext` with a four-lemma getD-bridge chain) so the master `n ≤ 67 ⟹ C(n,k) < 2^64` bound closes by `native_decide` on a quadratic computation instead of an exponential one, and four contract clauses (totality / k > n = 0 / Pascal recurrence / symmetry) project from a single `binomial_postcondition` closed form.
